vulnerability.reason: Generated describes lack of independent verification, while reference specifies low-resource language content creators facing disproportionate quality loss from rare linguistic pattern removal.
vulnerability.reference: Content creators in low-resource languages face disproportionate quality loss when unlearning removes rare linguistic patterns.
vulnerability.generated: Content creators lack independent verification of LLM output quality and rely entirely on model reliability, with no fallback when unlearning corrupts language patterns.

impact.reason: Generated impact affects content creators' productivity and credibility; reference impact is marginalized language communities losing access to tools, widening digital inequality.
impact.reference: Marginalized language communities lose access to functional generative AI tools, widening digital inequality.
impact.generated: Degraded text coherence and accuracy force content creators to spend extra effort correcting errors, damaging productivity and professional credibility.

dose_response.reason: Generated causal arc involves reliance and error-correction, missing the removal of functional language patterns disproportionately harming low-resource communities and amplifying misinformation.
dose_response.reference: Unlearning copyrighted content inadvertently removes functional language patterns, degrading outputs for content creators, which disproportionately harms low-resource language communities and amplifies misinformation.
dose_response.generated: LLM unlearning causing catastrophic forgetting and knowledge corruption, through content creators using unlearned LLM-based writing tools, triggers their reliance on model reliability without independent verification, leading to degraded text coherence and extra error-correction effort damaging productivity and professional credibility.
